description: Treat generated declarations and SDK types as contracts that must be regenerated or validated after source changes.
body: Apply when changes touch API schemas, protobuf/OpenAPI/GraphQL definitions, SDK source, build scripts, declarations, or package exports. The action is to find the generator command or checked-in generated artifacts, then verify whether generated types are stale. Do not hand-edit generated files unless the repository explicitly treats them as source. Verification signals include generator script output, changed .d.ts or SDK files, typecheck against consumers, and package export consistency. Risk boundary: stale generated types can make downstream packages compile against a contract that runtime code no longer honors. Use when TypeScript / JavaScript work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
